required: Test describes ONE behavior (single assertion focus)
Test uses descriptive name reflecting expected behavior
Test run executed and FAILS with expected error
Failure message is meaningful (not a generic assertion error)
red.gate: Do not proceed to GREEN until test failure confirmed with expected error

required: Implementation is MINIMAL — passes test, nothing more
No additional functionality beyond what the test requires
green.gate: Do not proceed to REFACTOR until test passes

required: Decision reported: refactor or skip with reason
If refactoring: changes improve clarity without altering behavior
Test run executed and still PASSES after refactoring
refactor.gate: Do not proceed to next AC until commit made for this TDD cycle

triggers: Test passes when it should fail (false positive)
Wrong error message or unexpected failure reason
Multiple tests fail unexpectedly after a change
Test is flaky (passes sometimes, fails sometimes)
steps: Isolate: run single test, not suite
Verify assertion: is the test checking the right thing?
Check setup/teardown: shared state leaking between tests?
Review test dependencies: external services, file system, network?
Simplify: reduce test to smallest reproducing case
If flaky: check for timing dependencies, async issues, or race conditions
